# :zap: Collection of Serverless Plugins :battery:

[![serverless](http://public.serverless.com/badges/v3.svg)](http://www.serverless.com)
[![Build Status](https://travis-ci.com/CoorpAcademy/serverless-plugins.svg?branch=master)](https://travis-ci.com/CoorpAcademy/serverless-plugins)
[![lerna](https://img.shields.io/badge/maintained%20with-lerna-cc00ff.svg)](https://lernajs.io/)


|  Plugin       | Version       |
| ------------- | ------------- |
| [`dynamodb-streams-readable`](/packages/dynamodb-streams-readable) | [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/dynamodb-streams-readable.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/dynamodb-streams-readable)|
| [`serverless-apigateway-access-logs`](/packages/serverless-apigateway-access-logs)| [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/serverless-apigateway-access-logs.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/serverless-apigateway-access-logs)|

addedLines: - architecture-analyst: Maps codebase architecture — directory structure, entry points, module boundaries, data flow, deployment topology. Use proactively when analyzing unfamiliar codebases or onboarding. (Tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, Write, Edit)

- aws-ops: AWS operations specialist for TLS infrastructure. Use for deployment, CloudWatch log analysis, DynamoDB inspection, SQS queue monitoring, S3 operations, and staging validation. Can deploy lambdas, tail logs, check queue depths, scan tables, and validate staging environments. (Tools: Bash, Read, Glob, Grep)
- builder: Generic engineering agent that executes ONE task at a time. Use when work needs to be done - writing code, creating files, implementing features. (Tools: All tools)
- claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- complexity-analyst: Finds complex files, cyclomatic complexity hotspots, tech debt markers, deeply nested logic, and god components. Use proactively for complexity audits. (Tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, Write, Edit)

- create-worktree-subagent: Use when the user explicitly asks for a SUBAGENT to create a worktree. If the user does not mention "subagent" or explicitly request subagent delegation, do NOT trigger this. Only use when user says things like "use a subagent to create a worktree" or "delegate worktree creation to a subagent". Creates isolated git worktrees with parallel-running configuration. (Tools: SlashCommand, Bash, Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep)

- datadog: Datadog log analyst for TLS pipeline. Use proactively when investigating production issues, analyzing job outcomes, debugging pipeline errors, querying Datadog logs, or building log-based reports. (Tools: Bash, Read, Write, Glob, Grep)

- debug-ci: CI/CD debugging specialist for AWS CodeBuild failures. Use proactively when the user mentions CI failure, build failure, tests failing in CI, CodeBuild error, or "passes locally but fails in CI". (Tools: Bash, Read, Grep, Glob)

- dependency-analyst: Analyzes package manifests, dependency graphs, version constraints, circular dependencies, outdated patterns, and bundle size concerns. Use proactively for dependency audits. (Tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, Write, Edit)

- docs-scraper: Documentation scraping specialist. Use proactively to fetch and save documentation from URLs as properly formatted markdown files. (Tools: mcp__firecrawl-mcp__firecrawl_scrape, WebFetch, Write, Edit)
- documentalist: Read-only documentation auditor. Explores either existing docs or source code and produces a structured inventory report. (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit, NotebookEdit)

- e2e-runner: E2E pipeline test runner for TLS staging validation. Executes a single test case -- sends the API request, polls DynamoDB for completion, verifies artifacts in S3, checks CloudWatch logs, and returns a structured pass/fail report. Use when running pipeline validation test cases. (Tools: Bash, Read, Write, Glob, Grep)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- fetch-docs-haiku45: Fetch and save documentation from URLs using Haiku 4.5 model. Use for benchmarking documentation retrieval tasks. (Tools: Write, Bash, Read, mcp__firecrawl-mcp__firecrawl_scrape, WebFetch)

- fetch-docs-sonnet45: Fetch and save documentation from URLs using Sonnet 4.5 model. Use for benchmarking documentation retrieval tasks. (Tools: Write, Bash, Read, mcp__firecrawl-mcp__firecrawl_scrape, WebFetch)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- jira: Jira ticket management specialist. Use proactively for any Jira operations -- creating tickets, fetching issue details, updating status, linking PRs, searching issues, adding comments, and generating EOD summaries. Delegates to this agent when the user mentions Jira, tickets, sprints, or CLXP issues. (Tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob)

- meta-architect: Expert agent architect that generates new subagents, skills, and commands for Claude Code. Use proactively when the user wants to create, update, or reorganize any extensibility component (agent, skill, command). Understands the 4-layer architecture (JUST, COMMAND, SUBAGENT, SKILL) and places each component in the correct layer following latest Anthropic guidelines. (Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Grep, Glob, Bash, WebFetch)

- panelist: Deliberation panel agent. Spawned by the /deliberate skill to analyze a topic from a specific cognitive mandate. Do NOT use proactively -- only spawned by the deliberate orchestrator. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, WebFetch)

- pattern-analyst: Identifies design patterns, coding conventions, anti-patterns, and type safety violations in codebases. Use proactively for code quality audits. (Tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, Write, Edit)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- review: Code review specialist for TLS project. Use for reviewing code changes before commits and PRs. Analyzes handler compliance, port patterns, io-ts validation, FP compliance, error handling, and TLS coding standards. Provides structured reports with actionable findings. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- scout-report-suggest: Use proactively to scout codebase issues, identify problem locations, and suggest resolutions. Specialist for read-only analysis and reporting without making changes. (Tools: Read, Glob, Grep)

- scout-report-suggest-fast: Use proactively to quickly scout codebase issues, identify problem locations, and suggest resolutions. Specialist for read-only analysis and reporting without making changes. (Tools: Read, Glob, Grep)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

- validator: Read-only validation agent that checks if a task was completed successfully. Use after a builder finishes to verify work meets acceptance criteria. (Tools: All tools except Write, Edit, NotebookEdit)

attachment.content:
- agents-sdk: Build AI agents on Cloudflare Workers using the Agents SDK. Load when creating stateful agents, durable workflows, real-time WebSocket apps, scheduled tasks, MCP servers, chat applications, voice agents, or browser automation. Covers Agent class, state management, callable RPC, Workflows, durable execution, queues, retries, observability, and React hooks. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
- cloudflare: Comprehensive Cloudflare platform skill covering Workers, Pages, storage (KV, D1, R2), AI (Workers AI, Vectorize, Agents SDK), feature flags (Flagship), networking (Tunnel, Spectrum), security (WAF, DDoS), and infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, Pulumi). Use for any Cloudflare development task. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
- cloudflare-email-service: Send and receive transactional emails with Cloudflare Email Service (Email Sending + Email Routing). Use when building email sending (Workers binding or REST API), email routing, Agents SDK email handling, or integrating email into any app — Workers, Node.js, Python, Go, etc. Also use for email deliverability, SPF/DKIM/DMARC, wrangler email setup, MCP email tools, or when a coding agent needs to send emails. Even for simple requests like "add email to my Worker" — this skill has critical config details.
- create-worktree-skill: Use when the user explicitly asks for a SKILL to create a worktree. If the user does not mention "skill" or explicitly request skill invocation, do NOT trigger this. Only use when user says things like "use a skill to create a worktree" or "invoke the worktree skill". Creates isolated git worktrees with parallel-running configuration.
- durable-objects: Create and review Cloudflare Durable Objects. Use when building stateful coordination (chat rooms, multiplayer games, booking systems), implementing RPC methods, SQLite storage, alarms, WebSockets, or reviewing DO code for best practices. Covers Workers integration, wrangler config, and testing with Vitest. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
- pptx-creator: Create, edit, and customize PowerPoint (PPTX) presentations. Use when user mentions PPT, PPTX, PowerPoint, slides, presentation, or asks to generate/edit/brand a presentation.
- sandbox-sdk: Build sandboxed applications for secure code execution. Load when building AI code execution, code interpreters, CI/CD systems, interactive dev environments, or executing untrusted code. Covers Sandbox SDK lifecycle, commands, files, code interpreter, and preview URLs. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
- turnstile-spin: Set up Cloudflare Turnstile end-to-end in a project — scan the codebase, create the widget via the Cloudflare API, deploy the managed siteverify Worker, write the frontend snippets, validate, and persist the skill. Load this when a user asks to add Turnstile, set up CAPTCHA, protect a form from bots, or fix a Turnstile integration. Mirrors developers.cloudflare.com/turnstile/spin.
- video-processor: Process video files with audio extraction, format conversion (mp4, webm), and Whisper transcription. Use when user mentions video conversion, audio extraction, transcription, mp4, webm, ffmpeg, or whisper transcription.
- web-perf: Analyzes web performance using Chrome DevTools MCP. Measures Core Web Vitals (LCP, INP, CLS) and supplementary metrics (FCP, TBT, Speed Index), identifies render-blocking resources, network dependency chains, layout shifts, caching issues, and accessibility gaps. Use when asked to audit, profile, debug, or optimize page load performance, Lighthouse scores, or site speed. Biases towards retrieval from current documentation over pre-trained knowledge.
- workers-best-practices: Reviews and authors Cloudflare Workers code against production best practices. Load when writing new Workers, reviewing Worker code, configuring wrangler.jsonc, or checking for common Workers anti-patterns (streaming, floating promises, global state, secrets, bindings, observability). Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
- worktree-manager-skill: Comprehensive git worktree management. Use when the user wants to create, remove, list, or manage worktrees. Handles all worktree operations including creation, deletion, and status checking.
- wrangler: Cloudflare Workers CLI for deploying, developing, and managing Workers, KV, R2, D1, Vectorize, Hyperdrive, Workers AI, Containers, Queues, Workflows, Pipelines, and Secrets Store. Load before running wrangler commands to ensure correct syntax and best practices. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
- analyze-codebase: Spawn 4 parallel analyst agents to produce a comprehensive codebase intelligence report
- bench:find_and_summarize: Finds and summarizes specific files in the codebase and saves the summary to app_docs directory
- bench:load_ai_docs: Fetch and save AI documentation using model-specific subagents for benchmarking
- bench:plan_new_feature: Creates a concise engineering implementation plan based on user requirements and saves it to specs directory
- brainstorm: Brainstorm ideas and solutions interactively
- build: Implement a plan using the builder agent
- convert_paths_absolute: Converts relative paths in .claude/settings.json command scripts to absolute paths
- create_worktree_prompt: Create a git worktree with isolated configuration for parallel development
- init-project-worktrees: Initialize worktree support in current project
- list_worktrees_prompt: List all git worktrees with their configuration and status
- load_ai_docs: Load documentation from their respective websites into local markdown files our agents can use as context.
- plan: Create detailed implementation plan with phases
- plan_w_team: Creates a concise engineering implementation plan based on user requirements and saves it to specs directory
- prime: Prime
- rebase: Rebase current branch onto target with conflict resolution
- remove_worktree_prompt: Remove a git worktree, delete its branch, and stop its running services
- t_metaprompt_workflow: Create a new prompt
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# Handover — CLXP-103: v4-ify `CoorpAcademy/serverless-plugins`, then retire serverless v3 (fixes CLXP-922 natively)
2	
3	> **Author:** automated session 2026-06-19 · **For:** the next engineer/agent picking up CLXP-103
4	> **One-line:** Update our own Serverless-Framework plugins for **Serverless Framework v4**, publish them, then bump the framework in the consuming services — which gets us **off EOL serverless v3** and makes the framework emit **node20+ custom-resource helpers natively** (the real fix for CLXP-922, replacing the interim hook in `coorpacademy-lambda` PR #1734).
5	
6	---
7	
8	## 0. TL;DR / start here
9	
10	1. **Most of the work already exists.** `CoorpAcademy/serverless-plugins` **PR #264** — *"Feat: update serverless-offline for s3 and sqs to support serverless v4"* — is **OPEN, MERGEABLE/CLEAN, Travis green** (community contributor `agnusha`, last updated 2025-09-30). It v4-fixes **serverless-offline-s3** and **serverless-offline-sqs**. **First action: review, validate, and merge it.**
11	2. It does **NOT** cover **serverless-offline-kinesis** and **serverless-offline-dynamodb-streams**, which import the same removed module — apply the identical fix to those two.
12	3. Then publish new majors of the 4 plugins (lerna), bump them + `serverless-offline@^14` + `serverless@^4` in the consuming services, verify, deploy (gated), and **close CLXP-922 + PR #1734** (the interim hook).
13	
14	⚠️ Two different "#264" in this saga — don't confuse them:
15	- **`serverless-plugins#264`** = the v4 plugin fix (this doc). 
16	- **`translated-language-service#264`** = the CLXP-924 DynamoDB billing-mode fix (unrelated).
17	
18	---
19	
20	## 1. Why now / context
21	
22	- **CLXP-922** needs 4 lambdas off `nodejs18.x`: `api-scorm-{prod,staging}-custom-resource-existing-s3` and `push-{prod,staging}-custom-resource-apigw-cw-role`. These are **framework-generated CloudFormation custom-resource helper lambdas**, not app code. Serverless **v3 hard-codes them to nodejs18.x** with no override (upstream `serverless#13096`). **v4 emits them on node20+** (`serverless@4.0.26`→node20, `@4.20+`→node22). So fixing 922 "properly" = getting onto v4.
23	- Getting onto v4 was blocked because **our own offline plugins crash under v4** (details §3). They're ours → CLXP-103 owns them.
24	- Bonus: serverless v3 is end-of-feature-life; this migration retires it across the lambda fleet.
25	- An **interim** already shipped for 922: `coorpacademy-lambda` **PR #1734** adds a v3 package-time hook (`.serverless_plugins/force-helper-runtime.js`) that rewrites the helper runtime to node20 — **green, tested, deploy-gated**. It's a stopgap to retire once this (103) lands.
26	
27	**Account lambda runtime spread (eu-west-1, 446570804799, 2026-06-19):** 251 node22 · 50 node20 · 14 python3.12 · 4 node18 (the helpers) · 1 node12 (mobile-store, CLXP-927). A full deployed-vs-source CSV is at `docs/cost-optimization/reports/2026-06-19-lambda-runtime-map.csv`.
28	
29	---
30	
31	## 2. The repo: `CoorpAcademy/serverless-plugins`
32	
33	- **Tooling:** lerna + yarn workspaces (`packages/*`, `tests/*`). Root scripts: `eslint`, `ava`, `nyc`, `test:unit`, `test:integration`, `test`, `publish`. CI: **Travis** (`.travis.yml`). Publish: lerna (`npm run publish`). Node engines `>=18` (`.nvmrc` 18.18.2). Default branch `master`.
34	- **Packages (6)** and their v4 status:
35	
36	| Package | Ver | Imports removed `@serverless/utils/log`? | v4 status |
37	|---|---|---|---|
38	| `serverless-offline-s3` | 7.0.0 | **yes** (`src/index.js`, `src/s3.js`) | **fixed in PR #264** |
39	| `serverless-offline-sqs` | 8.0.0 | **yes** (`src/index.js`, `src/sqs.js`) | **fixed in PR #264** |
40	| `serverless-offline-kinesis` | 7.0.0 | **yes** (1 file) | ❌ **TODO — same fix** |
41	| `serverless-offline-dynamodb-streams` | 7.0.0 | **yes** (1 file) | ❌ **TODO — same fix** |
42	| `dynamodb-streams-readable` | 3.0.0 | no | n/a |
43	| `serverless-apigateway-access-logs` | 2.0.0 | no | n/a (verify) |
44	
45	- **NOT in this repo:** `serverless-offline-ssm` (used by api-scorm/push) is a **third-party** package, not ours. It did **not** import the removed module in our testing, so it appears v4-tolerant — but **verify** it loads under v4 before declaring victory.
46	- Note PR #264's serverless **peer**: the plugins don't declare a `serverless` peer (only `serverless-offline >=11`); the v4 break is purely the **runtime `require`** of the removed module, not a peer-range block.
47	
48	---
49	
50	## 3. What breaks under v4, and the canonical fix
51	
52	**Root cause:** Serverless Framework v4 **removed the `@serverless/utils/log` module**. Our plugins do, at module top-level:
53	```js
54	const log = require('@serverless/utils/log').log;   // ❌ throws "Cannot find module" under v4
55	```
56	This crashes **`sls package`** and **`sls offline`** (the plugin is `require`d on every command).
57	
58	**The fix (exactly what PR #264 does — copy this pattern for kinesis + dynamodb-streams):** v4 **injects a logger as the plugin's 3rd constructor argument**. Stop importing the module; take `{log}` from the constructor and thread it into sub-modules, with a console fallback for safety:
59	```js
60	// before
61	const log = require('@serverless/utils/log').log;
62	class ServerlessOfflineS3 {
63	  constructor(serverless, cliOptions) { ... }
64	  ...
65	  this.serverless.cli.log('Halting offline server');
66	}
67	
68	// after (PR #264 pattern)
69	class ServerlessOfflineS3 {
70	  constructor(serverless, cliOptions, {log} = {}) {
71	    this.log = log || { notice: console.log.bind(console), debug: () => {}, warning: console.warn.bind(console) /* … */ };
72	    ...
73	  }
74	  ...
75	  this.log.notice('Halting offline server');
76	  // pass it down: new S3(this.lambda, resources, this.options, this.log)
77	}
78	// in s3.js / sqs.js: accept `log = defaultLog` in the constructor; use this.log.{notice,debug,warning}; a _safeLog() guard is used for optional levels.
79	```
80	Apply the **same** to `serverless-offline-kinesis` and `serverless-offline-dynamodb-streams` (1 file each).
81	
82	**Consumer-side cascade** (what we hit fixing `coorpacademy-lambda` for v4 — anticipate these in the services, NOT in the plugin repo):
83	1. `npm install` **ERESOLVE** — `serverless-offline@13` peers `serverless ^3`; bump consumers to **`serverless-offline@^14`** (peers `serverless ^4`). Our offline-* plugins peer `serverless-offline >=11`, so they're satisfied.
84	2. After install, `sls package`/`sls offline` crash on the removed module → **this is what merging the published v4 plugins fixes**.
85	3. `tsc` regressions in some services (`@types/lodash`, `@types/aws-lambda`) from v4's churned dependency tree — fix per-service (add/pin the missing `@types`).
86	4. If you commit any `patches/` (patch-package), api-scorm lints `**/*` and eslint chokes on `*.patch` → add `patches/` to `.eslintignore` (avoid this entirely by using published plugins, not patches).
87	5. **serverless v4 requires a license/login** (`SERVERLESS_ACCESS_KEY`) for `deploy` on the host/CI. Provision it before deploying. (`serverless package` did not prompt in our tests.)
88	
89	---
90	
91	## 4. Step-by-step plan
92	
93	1. **serverless-plugins repo**
94	   a. Review + validate **PR #264** (run `npm test` / the integration tests locally; it's MERGEABLE/CLEAN, Travis green). Merge.
95	   b. Apply the **same `{log}` 3rd-arg fix** to `serverless-offline-kinesis` and `serverless-offline-dynamodb-streams` (+ tests). Open a PR, get green, merge.
96	   c. Optionally widen peer to `serverless-offline: '^10 || >=11'` already present; no serverless peer needed.
97	   d. **Publish** new **major** versions of the 4 offline-* packages via lerna (canary first: `lerna publish --canary` or a prerelease tag, smoke-test in one service, then stable). Follow semver — these are behavior-preserving but a major signals the v4 requirement.
98	
99	2. **Consuming services** (`coorpacademy-lambda` first: `api-scorm`, `push`; then any using kinesis/dynamodb-streams — see §5)
100	   a. Per service: bump `serverless ^3.40.0 → ^4` (start `^4.0.26` for node20 helpers, or latest for node22), `serverless-offline ^13 → ^14`, and the offline-* plugins to the new majors. Regenerate lockfile.
101	   b. Fix any `tsc`/`@types` fallout (§3.3). Run the service's full `npm test` (lint + typecheck + unit + integration via `sls offline` + `sls package`).
102	   c. Confirm the generated custom-resource helpers now render **node20+** (`sls package` → inspect `.serverless/cloudformation-template-*.json`).
103	
104	3. **Deploy** (⚠️ `coorpacademy-lambda` has **no CI auto-deploy** — manual): provision `SERVERLESS_ACCESS_KEY`, then per service `AWS_PROFILE=coorp serverless deploy --force --stage staging` → verify → `--stage production`. Confirm in AWS the 4 helper lambdas report node20+.
105	
106	4. **Close out:** mark **CLXP-922** done; **close `coorpacademy-lambda` PR #1734** (the interim hook) and delete the `.serverless_plugins/force-helper-runtime.js` files; update **CLXP-103**.
107	
108	---
109	
110	## 5. Consumers / blast radius
111	
112	- **api-scorm** → `serverless-offline-s3` + `serverless-offline-ssm` (existing-S3 events on `scorm-{env}`/`scorm-bulk-{env}` → the `custom-resource-existing-s3` helper).
113	- **push** → `serverless-offline-sqs` + `serverless-offline-ssm` (API Gateway → the `custom-resource-apigw-cw-role` helper; note: the account CW role `serverlessApiGatewayCloudWatchRole` is already set, so push's helper is effectively a redundant no-op).
114	- **Others using kinesis / dynamodb-streams offline plugins:** run `env -u GITHUB_TOKEN gh search code "serverless-offline-kinesis" --owner CoorpAcademy` (and `-dynamodb-streams`, `-s3`, `-sqs`) to enumerate every consuming service/stage before the framework bump. The lambda-runtime CSV (`docs/cost-optimization/reports/2026-06-19-lambda-runtime-map.csv`) lists deployed vs source runtime per service to prioritize.
115	
116	---
117	
118	## 6. Guidelines & decisions
119	
120	- **Support v3 *and* v4 in the plugins** during the transition if feasible (the `{log}` fallback already degrades gracefully on v3 since `log` would be undefined → console fallback) so services can migrate one at a time. Validate the published plugin still works on a v3 service before mass-bumping.
121	- **Canary-publish first**, smoke-test in one service, then stable. Don't mass-bump consumers off an unproven plugin release.
122	- **TDD / keep the integration tests green** — PR #264 updates the `tests/serverless-plugins-integration` suite; mirror that for kinesis/dynamodb-streams.
123	- **Don't reintroduce patch-package or per-service hooks** — published v4 plugins make those unnecessary; the `force-helper-runtime` hook (#1734) is a temporary bridge only.
124	- **serverless v4 licensing:** ensure `SERVERLESS_ACCESS_KEY` exists wherever `serverless deploy` runs (dev hosts + any CI). Budget for this — it can silently block deploys.
125	- **Deploy is gated/manual** for `coorpacademy-lambda`; verify live AWS runtime after each deploy, never assume merge == deployed.
126	
127	---
128	
129	## 7. Acceptance criteria
130	
131	- [ ] serverless-plugins PR #264 merged; kinesis + dynamodb-streams given the same v4 fix + tests; all 4 offline-* packages **published** (new majors), zero `@serverless/utils/log` imports remaining in the repo.
132	- [ ] `serverless-offline-ssm` confirmed v4-OK (or replaced).
133	- [ ] api-scorm + push on `serverless@^4` + the new plugins; `npm test` green; `sls package` shows custom-resource helpers on **node20+**.
134	- [ ] Deployed (staging→prod) and **verified in AWS**: the 4 helper lambdas report node20+ (`aws lambda get-function-configuration`).
135	- [ ] CLXP-922 closed; `coorpacademy-lambda` PR #1734 closed and the `.serverless_plugins/force-helper-runtime.js` hook removed.
136	- [ ] Any other kinesis/dynamodb-streams consumers migrated or explicitly deferred with a note.
137	
138	---
139	
140	## 8. Risks & rollback
141	
142	- **Plugin behavior change under v4** (offline emulation): caught by the integration tests; canary-publish + per-service smoke test mitigates. Rollback = pin the consumer back to the v3 plugin major + `serverless@3`.
143	- **v4 framework behavior diffs** (variables resolver, config schema, packaging): validate `sls package` output diff per service before deploy.
144	- **Deploy regression:** these are manual deploys; deploy staging first, verify, and keep the prior version pinned for a fast revert. `coorpacademy-lambda` has no auto-rollback.
145	- **Licensing block:** missing `SERVERLESS_ACCESS_KEY` fails deploy — provision ahead of time.
146	
147	---
148	
149	## 9. Quick links
150	
151	- Repo: `CoorpAcademy/serverless-plugins` (lerna; packages: offline-s3/sqs/kinesis/dynamodb-streams, dynamodb-streams-readable, apigateway-access-logs)
152	- **serverless-plugins PR #264** — v4 fix for offline-s3/sqs (review + merge first): https://github.com/CoorpAcademy/serverless-plugins/pull/264
153	- Interim hook (to retire): **coorpacademy-lambda PR #1734** — https://github.com/CoorpAcademy/coorpacademy-lambda/pull/1734
154	- Tickets: **CLXP-103** (this work) · **CLXP-922** (the node18 helpers, fixed natively by this) · related: CLXP-924 (TLS dev), CLXP-927 (mobile-store node12), CLXP-910 (api-progression node20).
155	- Runtime map: `docs/cost-optimization/reports/2026-06-19-lambda-runtime-map.csv`

# Handover — CLXP-103: v4-ify `CoorpAcademy/serverless-plugins`, then retire serverless v3 (fixes CLXP-922 natively)

> **Author:** automated session 2026-06-19 · **For:** the next engineer/agent picking up CLXP-103
> **One-line:** Update our own Serverless-Framework plugins for **Serverless Framework v4**, publish them, then bump the framework in the consuming services — which gets us **off EOL serverless v3** and makes the framework emit **node20+ custom-resource helpers natively** (the real fix for CLXP-922, replacing the interim hook in `coorpacademy-lambda` PR #1734).

---

## 0. TL;DR / start here

1. **Most of the work already exists.** `CoorpAcademy/serverless-plugins` **PR #264** — *"Feat: update serverless-offline for s3 and sqs to support serverless v4"* — is **OPEN, MERGEABLE/CLEAN, Travis green** (community contributor `agnusha`, last updated 2025-09-30). It v4-fixes **serverless-offline-s3** and **serverless-offline-sqs**. **First action: review, validate, and merge it.**
2. It does **NOT** cover **serverless-offline-kinesis** and **serverless-offline-dynamodb-streams**, which import the same removed module — apply the identical fix to those two.
3. Then publish new majors of the 4 plugins (lerna), bump them + `serverless-offline@^14` + `serverless@^4` in the consuming services, verify, deploy (gated), and **close CLXP-922 + PR #1734** (the interim hook).

⚠️ Two different "#264" in this saga — don't confuse them:
- **`serverless-plugins#264`** = the v4 plugin fix (this doc). 
- **`translated-language-service#264`** = the CLXP-924 DynamoDB billing-mode fix (unrelated).

---

## 1. Why now / context

- **CLXP-922** needs 4 lambdas off `nodejs18.x`: `api-scorm-{prod,staging}-custom-resource-existing-s3` and `push-{prod,staging}-custom-resource-apigw-cw-role`. These are **framework-generated CloudFormation custom-resource helper lambdas**, not app code. Serverless **v3 hard-codes them to nodejs18.x** with no override (upstream `serverless#13096`). **v4 emits them on node20+** (`serverless@4.0.26`→node20, `@4.20+`→node22). So fixing 922 "properly" = getting onto v4.
- Getting onto v4 was blocked because **our own offline plugins crash under v4** (details §3). They're ours → CLXP-103 owns them.
- Bonus: serverless v3 is end-of-feature-life; this migration retires it across the lambda fleet.
- An **interim** already shipped for 922: `coorpacademy-lambda` **PR #1734** adds a v3 package-time hook (`.serverless_plugins/force-helper-runtime.js`) that rewrites the helper runtime to node20 — **green, tested, deploy-gated**. It's a stopgap to retire once this (103) lands.

**Account lambda runtime spread (eu-west-1, 446570804799, 2026-06-19):** 251 node22 · 50 node20 · 14 python3.12 · 4 node18 (the helpers) · 1 node12 (mobile-store, CLXP-927). A full deployed-vs-source CSV is at `docs/cost-optimization/reports/2026-06-19-lambda-runtime-map.csv`.

---

## 2. The repo: `CoorpAcademy/serverless-plugins`

- **Tooling:** lerna + yarn workspaces (`packages/*`, `tests/*`). Root scripts: `eslint`, `ava`, `nyc`, `test:unit`, `test:integration`, `test`, `publish`. CI: **Travis** (`.travis.yml`). Publish: lerna (`npm run publish`). Node engines `>=18` (`.nvmrc` 18.18.2). Default branch `master`.
- **Packages (6)** and their v4 status:

| Package | Ver | Imports removed `@serverless/utils/log`? | v4 status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `serverless-offline-s3` | 7.0.0 | **yes** (`src/index.js`, `src/s3.js`) | **fixed in PR #264** |
| `serverless-offline-sqs` | 8.0.0 | **yes** (`src/index.js`, `src/sqs.js`) | **fixed in PR #264** |
| `serverless-offline-kinesis` | 7.0.0 | **yes** (1 file) | ❌ **TODO — same fix** |
| `serverless-offline-dynamodb-streams` | 7.0.0 | **yes** (1 file) | ❌ **TODO — same fix** |
| `dynamodb-streams-readable` | 3.0.0 | no | n/a |
| `serverless-apigateway-access-logs` | 2.0.0 | no | n/a (verify) |

- **NOT in this repo:** `serverless-offline-ssm` (used by api-scorm/push) is a **third-party** package, not ours. It did **not** import the removed module in our testing, so it appears v4-tolerant — but **verify** it loads under v4 before declaring victory.
- Note PR #264's serverless **peer**: the plugins don't declare a `serverless` peer (only `serverless-offline >=11`); the v4 break is purely the **runtime `require`** of the removed module, not a peer-range block.

---

## 3. What breaks under v4, and the canonical fix

**Root cause:** Serverless Framework v4 **removed the `@serverless/utils/log` module**. Our plugins do, at module top-level:
```js
const log = require('@serverless/utils/log').log;   // ❌ throws "Cannot find module" under v4
```
This crashes **`sls package`** and **`sls offline`** (the plugin is `require`d on every command).

**The fix (exactly what PR #264 does — copy this pattern for kinesis + dynamodb-streams):** v4 **injects a logger as the plugin's 3rd constructor argument**. Stop importing the module; take `{log}` from the constructor and thread it into sub-modules, with a console fallback for safety:
```js
// before
const log = require('@serverless/utils/log').log;
class ServerlessOfflineS3 {
  constructor(serverless, cliOptions) { ... }
  ...
  this.serverless.cli.log('Halting offline server');
}

// after (PR #264 pattern)
class ServerlessOfflineS3 {
  constructor(serverless, cliOptions, {log} = {}) {
    this.log = log || { notice: console.log.bind(console), debug: () => {}, warning: console.warn.bind(console) /* … */ };
    ...
  }
  ...
  this.log.notice('Halting offline server');
  // pass it down: new S3(this.lambda, resources, this.options, this.log)
}
// in s3.js / sqs.js: accept `log = defaultLog` in the constructor; use this.log.{notice,debug,warning}; a _safeLog() guard is used for optional levels.
```
Apply the **same** to `serverless-offline-kinesis` and `serverless-offline-dynamodb-streams` (1 file each).

**Consumer-side cascade** (what we hit fixing `coorpacademy-lambda` for v4 — anticipate these in the services, NOT in the plugin repo):
1. `npm install` **ERESOLVE** — `serverless-offline@13` peers `serverless ^3`; bump consumers to **`serverless-offline@^14`** (peers `serverless ^4`). Our offline-* plugins peer `serverless-offline >=11`, so they're satisfied.
2. After install, `sls package`/`sls offline` crash on the removed module → **this is what merging the published v4 plugins fixes**.
3. `tsc` regressions in some services (`@types/lodash`, `@types/aws-lambda`) from v4's churned dependency tree — fix per-service (add/pin the missing `@types`).
4. If you commit any `patches/` (patch-package), api-scorm lints `**/*` and eslint chokes on `*.patch` → add `patches/` to `.eslintignore` (avoid this entirely by using published plugins, not patches).
5. **serverless v4 requires a license/login** (`SERVERLESS_ACCESS_KEY`) for `deploy` on the host/CI. Provision it before deploying. (`serverless package` did not prompt in our tests.)

---

## 4. Step-by-step plan

1. **serverless-plugins repo**
   a. Review + validate **PR #264** (run `npm test` / the integration tests locally; it's MERGEABLE/CLEAN, Travis green). Merge.
   b. Apply the **same `{log}` 3rd-arg fix** to `serverless-offline-kinesis` and `serverless-offline-dynamodb-streams` (+ tests). Open a PR, get green, merge.
   c. Optionally widen peer to `serverless-offline: '^10 || >=11'` already present; no serverless peer needed.
   d. **Publish** new **major** versions of the 4 offline-* packages via lerna (canary first: `lerna publish --canary` or a prerelease tag, smoke-test in one service, then stable). Follow semver — these are behavior-preserving but a major signals the v4 requirement.

2. **Consuming services** (`coorpacademy-lambda` first: `api-scorm`, `push`; then any using kinesis/dynamodb-streams — see §5)
   a. Per service: bump `serverless ^3.40.0 → ^4` (start `^4.0.26` for node20 helpers, or latest for node22), `serverless-offline ^13 → ^14`, and the offline-* plugins to the new majors. Regenerate lockfile.
   b. Fix any `tsc`/`@types` fallout (§3.3). Run the service's full `npm test` (lint + typecheck + unit + integration via `sls offline` + `sls package`).
   c. Confirm the generated custom-resource helpers now render **node20+** (`sls package` → inspect `.serverless/cloudformation-template-*.json`).

3. **Deploy** (⚠️ `coorpacademy-lambda` has **no CI auto-deploy** — manual): provision `SERVERLESS_ACCESS_KEY`, then per service `AWS_PROFILE=coorp serverless deploy --force --stage staging` → verify → `--stage production`. Confirm in AWS the 4 helper lambdas report node20+.

4. **Close out:** mark **CLXP-922** done; **close `coorpacademy-lambda` PR #1734** (the interim hook) and delete the `.serverless_plugins/force-helper-runtime.js` files; update **CLXP-103**.

---

## 5. Consumers / blast radius

- **api-scorm** → `serverless-offline-s3` + `serverless-offline-ssm` (existing-S3 events on `scorm-{env}`/`scorm-bulk-{env}` → the `custom-resource-existing-s3` helper).
- **push** → `serverless-offline-sqs` + `serverless-offline-ssm` (API Gateway → the `custom-resource-apigw-cw-role` helper; note: the account CW role `serverlessApiGatewayCloudWatchRole` is already set, so push's helper is effectively a redundant no-op).
- **Others using kinesis / dynamodb-streams offline plugins:** run `env -u GITHUB_TOKEN gh search code "serverless-offline-kinesis" --owner CoorpAcademy` (and `-dynamodb-streams`, `-s3`, `-sqs`) to enumerate every consuming service/stage before the framework bump. The lambda-runtime CSV (`docs/cost-optimization/reports/2026-06-19-lambda-runtime-map.csv`) lists deployed vs source runtime per service to prioritize.

---

## 6. Guidelines & decisions

- **Support v3 *and* v4 in the plugins** during the transition if feasible (the `{log}` fallback already degrades gracefully on v3 since `log` would be undefined → console fallback) so services can migrate one at a time. Validate the published plugin still works on a v3 service before mass-bumping.
- **Canary-publish first**, smoke-test in one service, then stable. Don't mass-bump consumers off an unproven plugin release.
- **TDD / keep the integration tests green** — PR #264 updates the `tests/serverless-plugins-integration` suite; mirror that for kinesis/dynamodb-streams.
- **Don't reintroduce patch-package or per-service hooks** — published v4 plugins make those unnecessary; the `force-helper-runtime` hook (#1734) is a temporary bridge only.
- **serverless v4 licensing:** ensure `SERVERLESS_ACCESS_KEY` exists wherever `serverless deploy` runs (dev hosts + any CI). Budget for this — it can silently block deploys.
- **Deploy is gated/manual** for `coorpacademy-lambda`; verify live AWS runtime after each deploy, never assume merge == deployed.

---

## 7. Acceptance criteria

- [ ] serverless-plugins PR #264 merged; kinesis + dynamodb-streams given the same v4 fix + tests; all 4 offline-* packages **published** (new majors), zero `@serverless/utils/log` imports remaining in the repo.
- [ ] `serverless-offline-ssm` confirmed v4-OK (or replaced).
- [ ] api-scorm + push on `serverless@^4` + the new plugins; `npm test` green; `sls package` shows custom-resource helpers on **node20+**.
- [ ] Deployed (staging→prod) and **verified in AWS**: the 4 helper lambdas report node20+ (`aws lambda get-function-configuration`).
- [ ] CLXP-922 closed; `coorpacademy-lambda` PR #1734 closed and the `.serverless_plugins/force-helper-runtime.js` hook removed.
- [ ] Any other kinesis/dynamodb-streams consumers migrated or explicitly deferred with a note.

---

## 8. Risks & rollback

- **Plugin behavior change under v4** (offline emulation): caught by the integration tests; canary-publish + per-service smoke test mitigates. Rollback = pin the consumer back to the v3 plugin major + `serverless@3`.
- **v4 framework behavior diffs** (variables resolver, config schema, packaging): validate `sls package` output diff per service before deploy.
- **Deploy regression:** these are manual deploys; deploy staging first, verify, and keep the prior version pinned for a fast revert. `coorpacademy-lambda` has no auto-rollback.
- **Licensing block:** missing `SERVERLESS_ACCESS_KEY` fails deploy — provision ahead of time.

---

## 9. Quick links

- Repo: `CoorpAcademy/serverless-plugins` (lerna; packages: offline-s3/sqs/kinesis/dynamodb-streams, dynamodb-streams-readable, apigateway-access-logs)
- **serverless-plugins PR #264** — v4 fix for offline-s3/sqs (review + merge first): https://github.com/CoorpAcademy/serverless-plugins/pull/264
- Interim hook (to retire): **coorpacademy-lambda PR #1734** — https://github.com/CoorpAcademy/coorpacademy-lambda/pull/1734
- Tickets: **CLXP-103** (this work) · **CLXP-922** (the node18 helpers, fixed natively by this) · related: CLXP-924 (TLS dev), CLXP-927 (mobile-store node12), CLXP-910 (api-progression node20).
- Runtime map: `docs/cost-optimization/reports/2026-06-19-lambda-runtime-map.csv`
- Auth: GitHub `env -u GITHUB_TOKEN gh …` · AWS `uvx --from awscli aws … --profile coorp --region eu-west-1` (acct 446570804799).

sudo: required
language: node_js

cache: npm

content.text:
# Prime + Critical Review: serverless-plugins v4 migration

**Read-only.** Nothing modified. `ai_docs/README.md` doesn't exist, so that prime step was a no-op.

## What this repo is

Lerna + yarn-workspaces monorepo of 6 Serverless Framework plugins. The four `serverless-offline-*` plugins (s3, sqs, kinesis, dynamodb-streams) are near-identical: each is a class with `offline:start:*` hooks that spins up local emulators (Minio / ElasticMQ / kinesalite) and routes events into `serverless-offline`'s Lambda runner. All four `require('@serverless/utils/log')` at module top — the exact module v4 deleted. That single `require` throws on *every* `sls` command under v4. That's the whole bug.

## The core fix is correct — don't throw the PR out

agnusha's direction is right and idiomatic: stop importing the dead module, take `{log}` from the **3rd constructor argument** (the framework injects it), thread it into sub-modules, console-fallback for safety. Also correctly swaps the **other** dead v4 API, `this.serverless.cli.log(...)` → `this.log.notice(...)`, and fixes a latent bug (`log.warn` → `log.warning`; `.warn` never existed on this logger). Keep the architecture. **But do not merge as-is** — here's what I'd challenge.

## The five real problems

**1. 🔴 CI does not actually test serverless v4 — "Travis green" is a false signal.**
Root `package.json` still pins `serverless: ^3.36.0`. `.travis.yml` only bumps node `20`→`20.18.1` — no v4 lane. The integration tests run `sls offline` against the **root's serverless v3**, while the workspace bumps `serverless-offline` to `^14` (which peers serverless `^4`). So green proves "works under v3 + offline@14," **not** the thing the PR claims. The entire v4 fix is unverified by CI, and the dependency tree is internally inconsistent (offline@14 wants sls@4, root has sls@3 — that's most of the 21k-line lockfile churn). *Owning this means adding a real `serverless@4` test matrix before trusting any green check.*

**2. 🔴 Peer-range narrowing breaks the gradual-migration strategy the handover mandates.**
PR sets `serverless-offline: "^14.4.0 || >=14"` (which is just `>=14`, redundantly written) and dev-dep `^14.4.0` — **dropping** offline 10–13 support. But the `{log}` 3rd arg exists in **both serverless v3 (≥3.0) and v4**, and the code degrades gracefully when it's absent. So the plugin is *backward-compatible by construction*. Handover §6 explicitly wants v3+v4 support so services migrate one at a time — this peer change forces a hard cutover and contradicts that. **Keep the peer wide** (`>=11`, or `>=10`). If peer stays wide, these may not even need to be major bumps.

**3. 🟠 The test rewrite silently weakens its own assertions.**
s3 `EXPECTED_LAMBDA_CALL` drops 9→7. The old test counted *lambda invocations* (`Billed Duration` lines) and explicitly expected pictures to be "consumed twice, by myPromiseHandler and myPythonHandler." The new test counts *unique events* via a `processedEvents` Set keyed `bucket-objectkey` — which **collapses those two handler invocations into one**. A regression where the second handler stops firing would now pass undetected. The counting also moved stderr→stdout with hand-rolled JSON scraping. This looks like "make the flaky test pass," not "validate behavior."

**4. 🟠 Tests now lean on hardcoded `delay(1000/2000)` sleeps + kill-on-count.** Inherently racy; will flake in CI. "Rethink everything" → poll for completion (or assert on emulator/queue state) instead of fixed sleeps.

**5. 🟡 DRY + defensive-code inconsistency.** The console-shim `{debug, notice, warning}` is duplicated **3× per package** (inline in index.js, `defaultLog` in the sub-module, plus a `_safeLog` guard). In s3.js it's applied unevenly — `this.log.debug(...)` direct in `start()` but `_safeLog('warning', ...)` in `_s3Event`. Either normalize once in the constructor and trust it, or guard everywhere. The shim is also incomplete (no `error`/`info`/`success`). In a lerna monorepo this should be **one shared `default-log` util**.

**Plus the known gap:** the PR fixes only s3 + sqs. **kinesis and dynamodb-streams still `require('@serverless/utils/log')` and still call `serverless.cli.log`** (confirmed: 6 dead imports, 12 dead `cli.log` calls remain on master). Merging #264 alone leaves the repo half-migrated with mismatched peer ranges across packages.

## Recommended path (since we own it)

1. **Adopt agnusha's core `{log}` pattern, reject the peripheral churn.** Cherry-pick the logging fix; drop the test rewrite and peer narrowing.
2. **Factor one shared log-shim** (full level set) and apply it uniformly across all **four** packages in a single coherent change — not 2-now-2-later.
3. **Keep peers wide** (`serverless-offline: >=11`) so v3 services keep working; bump as **minor** unless we deliberately choose to drop old offline.
4. **Add a real v4 CI lane** (test matrix install of `serverless@4` + `serverless-offline@14`) — that's the only thing that actually proves the fix.
5. **Fix tests properly**: restore the per-handler invocation assertion (don't dedup it away), replace fixed sleeps with polling.
6. Canary-publish, smoke-test one consumer, then stable — per handover.

The net: agnusha got the *hard part* (the v4 logger contract) right and that saves us real work. What's not safe to take on faith is the **green checkmark**, the **peer range**, and the **test assertions** — all three are weaker than they look.

---

input.content:
# Serverless Framework v4 migration — critical review

> **Date:** 2026-06-19 · **Scope:** `CoorpAcademy/serverless-plugins` · **Context:** CLXP-103
> **Status:** read-only analysis. No code changed.
> Companion: [`open-pr-triage.md`](./open-pr-triage.md).

## TL;DR

agnusha's **PR #264** gets the *hard part* right — the v4 logger contract — and that saves us real
work. But it is **not safe to merge as-is**. Three things are weaker than they look: the **green CI
check** (it never runs serverless v4), the **peer range** (needlessly breaks gradual migration), and
the **test assertions** (silently weakened). Adopt the core fix, reject the peripheral churn, finish
the other two packages, and add a real v4 test lane.

---

## 1. What this repo is

Lerna + yarn-workspaces monorepo of 6 Serverless Framework plugins. The four `serverless-offline-*`
plugins (s3, sqs, kinesis, dynamodb-streams) are near-identical: a class with `offline:start:*` hooks
that spins up a local emulator (Minio / ElasticMQ / kinesalite) and routes events into
`serverless-offline`'s Lambda runner.

All four `require('@serverless/utils/log')` at module top — the **exact module v4 deleted**. That
single `require` throws on *every* `sls` command under v4. That is the whole bug.

## 2. The core bug & the correct fix

**Root cause:** v4 removed `@serverless/utils/log`.

```js
const log = require('@serverless/utils/log').log;   // ❌ "Cannot find module" under v4
```

**Correct fix (agnusha's direction — keep it):** stop importing the dead module; take `{log}` from
the **3rd constructor argument** (the framework injects it), thread it into sub-modules, with a
console fallback.

```js
constructor(serverless, cliOptions, {log} = {}) {
  this.log = log || { notice: console.log, debug: () => {}, warning: console.warn };
  ...
}
```

The PR also correctly swaps the **other** dead v4 API — `this.serverless.cli.log(...)` →
`this.log.notice(...)` — and fixes a latent bug (`log.warn` → `log.warning`; `.warn` never existed
on this logger).

> **Why this is backward-compatible:** the `{log}` 3rd arg has existed since **serverless v3.0**, and
> the code degrades to console when it's absent. So the plugin works on **both v3 and v4** by
> construction. This fact drives several decisions below.

## 3. What to challenge in PR #264

### 🔴 3.1 CI does not actually test serverless v4 — "Travis green" is a false signal
- Root `package.json` still pins `serverless: ^3.36.0`.
- `.travis.yml` only bumps node `20` → `20.18.1`. **No v4 lane.**
- Integration tests run `sls offline` against the **root's serverless v3**, while the workspace
  bumps `serverless-offline` to `^14` (which peers serverless `^4`).
- ⇒ Green proves *"works under v3 + offline@14"*, **not** the thing the PR claims. The dependency
  tree is internally inconsistent (offline@14 wants sls@4, root has sls@3) — that mismatch is most
  of the **21,305-line lockfile churn**.

**Action:** add a real `serverless@4` test matrix. That is the only thing that proves the fix.

### 🔴 3.2 Peer-range narrowing breaks the gradual-migration strategy
- PR sets `serverless-offline: "^14.4.0 || >=14"` (which is just `>=14`, redundantly written) and
  dev-dep `^14.4.0` — **dropping** offline 10–13 support.
- But the code is backward-compatible (§2). The handover **explicitly** wants v3+v4 support so
  services migrate one at a time.

**Action:** keep the peer **wide** (`serverless-offline: >=11`). If peer stays wide, these may not
even need to be **major** bumps.

### 🟠 3.3 The test rewrite silently weakens its own assertions
- s3 `EXPECTED_LAMBDA_CALL` drops **9 → 7**. The old test counted *lambda invocations*
  (`Billed Duration` lines) and expected pictures to be "consumed twice, by myPromiseHandler and
  myPythonHandler."
- The new test counts *unique events* via a `processedEvents` Set keyed `bucket-objectkey`, which
  **collapses those two handler invocations into one**. A regression where the second handler stops
  firing would now pass undetected.

**Action:** restore the per-handler invocation assertion; don't dedup it away.

### 🟠 3.4 Tests now lean on hardcoded sleeps + kill-on-count
- Rewritten tests use fixed `delay(1000/2000)` + process kill on a counter. Inherently racy; will
  flake in CI. Looks like "make the flaky test pass," not "validate behavior."

**Action:** poll for completion (or assert on emulator/queue state) instead of fixed sleeps.

### 🟡 3.5 DRY + inconsistent defensive code
- The console-shim `{debug, notice, warning}` is duplicated **3× per package** (inline in index.js,
  `defaultLog` in the sub-module, plus a `_safeLog` guard).
- In `s3.js` it's applied unevenly — `this.log.debug(...)` direct in `start()` but
  `_safeLog('warning', ...)` in `_s3Event`. Either normalize once in the constructor and trust it,
  or guard everywhere.
- The shim is also **incomplete** (no `error` / `info` / `success` levels).

**Action:** one shared `default-log` util across all four packages.

### ⚠️ 3.6 The PR is incomplete
- It fixes only **s3 + sqs**. **kinesis** and **dynamodb-streams** still
  `require('@serverless/utils/log')` (6 dead imports) and still call `serverless.cli.log` (12 dead
  calls). Merging #264 alone leaves the repo half-migrated with mismatched peer ranges across
  packages.

## 4. Recommended path (we own it now)

1. **Adopt agnusha's core `{log}` pattern, reject the peripheral churn** — cherry-pick the logging
   fix; drop the test rewrite and the peer narrowing.
2. **Factor one shared log-shim** (full level set) and apply it uniformly across **all four**
   packages in a single coherent change.
3. **Keep peers wide** (`serverless-offline: >=11`) so v3 services keep working; bump as **minor**
   unless we deliberately choose to drop old offline.
4. **Add a real v4 CI lane** (matrix install of `serverless@4` + `serverless-offline@14`).
5. **Fix tests properly** — restore per-handler assertions, replace fixed sleeps with polling.
6. **Canary-publish**, smoke-test one consumer, then stable (per handover).

## 5. Verified facts (grounding)

| Claim | Evidence |
|---|---|
| Dead import in all 4 plugins | `grep @serverless/utils/log packages/` → 6 hits (s3 ×2, sqs ×2, kinesis ×1, dynamodb ×1) |
| Dead `serverless.cli.log` in all 4 | `grep cli.log packages/` → 12 hits across the 4 index.js |
| PR diff size | `+21,750 / −14,290`, 15 files — **21,305/14,130 is the lockfile alone** |
| Peer change | `^10.0.2 \|\| >=11` → `^14.4.0 \|\| >=14` in s3 & sqs package.json |
| CI change | `.travis.yml`: node `"20"` → `"20.18.1"` only; no serverless@4 |
| Test weakening | `test-s3.js`: `EXPECTED_LAMBDA_CALL` 9 → 7; counting moved stderr→stdout w/ dedup Set |
| `{log}` exists on v3 too | serverless ≥3.0 plugin constructor 3rd arg `{log, writeText, progress}` |
</content>

# Open PR triage — `serverless-plugins`

> **Date:** 2026-06-19 · **17 open PRs** (123 closed, all-time) · **Status:** read-only analysis.
> Companion: [`serverless-v4-migration-review.md`](./serverless-v4-migration-review.md).

## How to read this

Each PR was judged on its **actual diff against current `master`**, not on title. The recurring
theme: most community PRs are old (2019–2023) and target an **architecture that no longer exists**
(the plugins were rewritten — `functionHelper.createHandler` / `LambdaContext` / `this.service` /
`before:offline:start` hooks are gone; current code uses `offline:start:init` hooks,
`import('serverless-offline/lambda')`, `_getEvents`/`_resolveFn`). Their patches are dead even when
the *idea* is still good.

**Buckets:** `MERGE` (after v4 base) · `MERGE+` (small work) · `REIMPL` (good idea, dead patch — close & redo) · `CLOSE` (throw away).

## Summary table

| PR | Title | Pkg | Size | State | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| #166 | fix undefined `this.region` | sqs | +1/-1 | conflict | **MERGE** — real bug, still on master |
| #264 | v4 support (s3 + sqs) | s3,sqs | +21k/-14k | clean | **MERGE+** — base, needs rework (see review doc) |
| #253 | distinct SQS delete-batch IDs | sqs | +14k/-3 | clean | **MERGE+** — strip lockfile, keep fix |
| #249 | kinesis: await lambda create | kinesis | +2/-2 | clean | **MERGE+** — take `await`, reject import change |
| #211 | sqs: queueName override from config | sqs | +7/-0 | clean | **MERGE+** — niche, fix param mutation |
| #98 | dynamodb: "table has no streams" error | ddb | +3/-0 | conflict | **REIMPL** — good DX, dead patch |
| #100 | dynamodb: retries option | ddb | +14/-1 | conflict | **REIMPL** — useful, dead patch |
| #183 | sqs: autocreate DLQ from resources | sqs | +57/-2 | conflict | **REIMPL** — real feature, dead + needs v4 |
| #168 | start non-SQS lambdas / default localhost | sqs | +13/-4 | conflict | **REIMPL** — partial, redo carefully |
| #99 | dynamodb: log function errors to console | ddb | +2/-1 | conflict | **CLOSE** — superseded by #264 logger |
| #117 | sqs: `enabled` flag | sqs | +17/-10 | conflict | **CLOSE** — dead arch, low value |
| #137 | kinesis: avoid waiting for termination | kinesis | +1/-2 | conflict | **CLOSE** — dead arch, unclear value |
| #234 | ⬆️ bump qs and formidable | dev | +14/-41 | clean | **CLOSE** — mooted by v4 lockfile regen |
| #243 | ⬆️ bump cookiejar | dev | +6/-3 | clean | **CLOSE** — mooted by v4 lockfile regen |
| #244 | ⬆️ bump simple-git | dev | +6/-3 | conflict | **CLOSE** — mooted by v4 lockfile regen |
| #246 | ⬆️ bump http-cache-semantics | dev | +6/-3 | clean | **CLOSE** — mooted by v4 lockfile regen |
| #240 | ⬆️ bump json5 | dev | +12/-6 | conflict | **CLOSE** — mooted by v4 lockfile regen |

---

## Details

### ✅ MERGE — real fix, do first

**#166 — fix undefined reference to `this.region`** (zlalvani)
> `sqs.js:119` on master: `new SQSEvent(messages, this.region, arn)`. The class stores `this.options`,
> never `this.region`, so **region is `undefined` today**. Fix: `this.region` → `this.options.region`.
> One line, genuine bug, still present. Rebase the one-liner onto the v4 base and ship.

### 🔧 MERGE+ — merge after small work

**#264 — v4 support** (agnusha) — the base. See [review doc](./serverless-v4-migration-review.md):
adopt core `{log}` fix, keep peers wide, finish kinesis+dynamodb, add v4 CI, fix tests. **Don't merge raw.**

**#253 — distinct SQS delete-batch IDs** (flipscholtz)
> AWS `deleteMessageBatch` requires entry `Id`s unique *within a batch*; current code reuses
> `MessageId` as `Id`. Real hardening (uuid per entry). The `+14,013` is a **brand-new per-package
> lockfile** committed by mistake — **drop the lockfile**, keep the ~3-line code change + `uuid` dep.

**#249 — kinesis await lambda create** (gabsong)
> Two changes: (a) `await this.lambda.create(...)` — **valid**, current code doesn't await; (b)
> swap dynamic `import('serverless-offline/lambda')` for `require('serverless-offline/src/lambda')`
> — **wrong** (reaches into `/src`, kills the deliberate ESM dynamic import). Take (a), reject (b).

**#211 — sqs queueName override** (mfamilia)
> Clean, targets current `_create`. Lets `custom.serverless-offline-sqs.queueName` override the event
> definition. Niche but harmless. Cleanup: it **mutates `rawSqsEventDefinition`** in place — make it
> non-mutating. Low priority.

### ♻️ REIMPL — keep the idea, close the PR

These patches no longer apply (dead architecture and/or v4-incompatible imports). Re-implement small
issues against current code; close the PR with a thank-you + link to the new work.

- **#98 — "table does not have streams" error** (dolsem): great DX (clear failure instead of a cryptic
  crash). Re-add an explicit guard in current dynamodb-streams flow.
- **#100 — retries option** (dolsem): default `Infinity` (current behavior), allow finite `retries`.
  Useful for local dev. Re-implement in current event loop + document.
- **#183 — autocreate DLQ from resources** (JeisonJHA): real feature (honor `RedrivePolicy` on
  autocreate). Patch uses dead `serverless-offline/dist/serverlessLog` import and old sqs.js shape —
  redo for current code **after** v4 base lands.
- **#168 — start lambdas without SQS events / default localhost** (russellpekala): partially valid
  (create the Lambda runner even when no SQS events). But the `this.options.host = 'localhost'`
  mutation and `lambda.start()` additions need scrutiny against current `_createLambda`. Redo cleanly.

### 🗑️ CLOSE — throw away

- **#99 — log function errors to console** (dolsem): **superseded** by #264 threading the real logger
  + the s3/sqs error-logging improvements. Obsolete.
- **#117 — `enabled` flag** (fernyettheplant): targets the old `before:offline:start` hook
  architecture and `this.streams`; none of it exists. Low value (just don't add the plugin). Close;
  re-implement only if a consumer actually needs it.
- **#137 — avoid waiting for termination signal** (umairnadeem): references `_startWithExplicitEnd`
  (gone; current is `_startWithReady`). Behavior intent is murky and risks breaking clean shutdown.
  Close.
- **#234 / #240 / #243 / #244 / #246 — dependabot bumps** (2023): all transitive/dev bumps against
  the **old** lockfile. The v4 work **regenerates the entire lockfile** (21k lines), mooting them;
  #240 and #244 already conflict. Close all five and let dependabot re-open against the new tree if
  anything is still flagged.

---

## Suggested execution order

1. **Land the v4 base** (reworked #264 across all 4 packages + wide peers + v4 CI). Everything else rebases on this.
2. **#166** (one-line region bug) and **#253** (delete-batch IDs, lockfile stripped) — quick, high-value correctness.
3. **#249(a)** await-create for kinesis while it's being v4-fixed anyway.
4. **#211** queueName override (optional, non-mutating).
5. **Re-implement** #98, #100, #183, #168 as fresh small PRs/issues against current code.
6. **Close** #99, #117, #137 and the 5 dependabot PRs with a short rationale.

> Net: of 17 open PRs — **1 to merge, 4 to merge after small work, 4 to re-implement, 8 to close**
> (3 stale-code + 5 dependabot).
</content>

content.text:
Created `docs/` with three files. The triage is grounded in each PR's **actual diff against current `master`**, not titles — which surfaced that most community PRs are dead patches against a rewritten architecture.

## What I wrote

- **`docs/README.md`** — index.
- **`docs/serverless-v4-migration-review.md`** — the v4 / PR #264 review from before, with a verified-facts table.
- **`docs/open-pr-triage.md`** — all 17 PRs bucketed.

## Triage headline: 17 PRs → **1 merge · 4 merge+ · 4 reimplement · 8 close**

| Verdict | PRs |
|---|---|
| ✅ **MERGE** (real fix, do first) | **#166** — `this.region` is `undefined` on master *right now* (`sqs.js:119`); one-line fix still applies |
| 🔧 **MERGE+** (small work) | **#264** (v4 base, rework), **#253** (drop the stray 14k lockfile, keep uuid fix), **#249** (take the `await`, reject the bad `/src` import), **#211** (queueName override, de-mutate) |
| ♻️ **REIMPL** (good idea, dead patch) | **#98** (no-streams error), **#100** (retries opt), **#183** (DLQ autocreate), **#168** (start lambdas w/o SQS) |
| 🗑️ **CLOSE** | **#99** (superseded by #264), **#117**/**#137** (dead architecture), and **all 5 dependabot** (#234/#240/#243/#244/#246 — mooted by the v4 lockfile regen) |

**Why so many closes:** the plugins were rewritten. PRs #98/#99/#100/#117/#137 target `functionHelper.createHandler`, `LambdaContext`, `this.service`, `before:offline:start` hooks — none of which exist anymore. The *ideas* survive; the patches don't.

**Suggested order:** land reworked v4 base → #166 + #253 → #249 await → optional #211 → re-implement the 4 → close the 8.

Want me to (a) draft the consolidated 4-package v4 implementation PR, or (b) open GitHub issues for the 4 re-implement items so the closes have somewhere to point? Still read-only until you say go.

---

## Audio Summary for Silou

Silou, I created a docs folder with the v4 review and a full triage of all seventeen open PRs. The headline: only one is a clean merge, four need small work, four are good ideas with dead patches to re-implement, and eight should be closed including all five dependabot bumps. And heads up, PR 166 fixes a region bug that's still live on master right now.

version: "3"
services:

  dynamodb:
    image: amazon/dynamodb-local:latest
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
  dynamodb-create:
    image: amazon/aws-cli:latest
    links:
      - dynamodb
    entrypoint: sh
    command: create-tables.sh
    volumes:
      - ./scripts/create-tables.sh:/aws/create-tables.sh:ro
    environment:
      - AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=local
      - AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=local
      - AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=eu-west-1
      - AWS_ENDPOINT_URL=http://dynamodb:8000

  sqs:
    image: softwaremill/elasticmq-native:latest
    ports:
      - "9324:9324"
    command: "-Dnode-address.host=\"*\""
  sqs-create:
    image: amazon/aws-cli:latest
    links:
      - sqs
    entrypoint: sh
    command: create-queues.sh
    volumes:
      - ./scripts/create-queues.sh:/aws/create-queues.sh:ro
    environment:
      - AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=local
      - AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=local
      - AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=eu-west-1
      - AWS_ENDPOINT_URL=http://sqs:9324

  kinesis:
    image: instructure/kinesalite:latest
    ports:
      - "4567:4567"
  kinesis-create:
    image: amazon/aws-cli:latest
    links:
      - kinesis
    entrypoint: sh
    command: create-streams.sh
    volumes:
      - ./scripts/create-streams.sh:/aws/create-streams.sh:ro
    environment:
      - AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=local
      - AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=local
      - AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=eu-west-1
      - AWS_ENDPOINT_URL=http://kinesis:4567
  s3:
    image: minio/minio:latest
    command: server /data
    ports:
      - "9000:9000"
    environment:
      - AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=local
      - AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=locallocal
      - MINIO_ACCESS_KEY=local
      - MINIO_SECRET_KEY=locallocal
      - AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=eu-west-1
      - AWS_ENDPOINT_URL=http://s3:9000

  s3-create:
    image: amazon/aws-cli:latest
    links:
      - s3
    entrypoint: sh
    command: create-buckets.sh
    volumes:
      - ./scripts/create-buckets.sh:/aws/create-buckets.sh:ro
    environment:
      - AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=local
      - AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=locallocal
      - AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=eu-west-1
      - AWS_ENDPOINT_URL=http://s3:9000

Key gating reality discovered: **the offline plugins have zero unit tests** — `ava.config.js` only globs `packages/**/test/*.js`, and only `dynamodb-streams-readable` has a `test/` dir. All offline-plugin testing is docker+serverless integration. So real "ownership" means **adding docker-free unit tests** for the logic I touch. `npm run eslint` (includes prettier via the coorpacademy config) is my fast, reliable gate.

Let me set up task tracking, then launch the analysis workflow (Step 1).

content.text:
# Workflow underway — ownership of `serverless-plugins`

I've structured your 5-step ask into a tracked, multi-workflow execution. Here's the plan and where it stands.

## The goal (Step 3, locked)
**Own this repo entirely.** Re-implement — as our own correct code — the v4 migration (A) and every worthwhile community-PR idea (B), with our own tests, docs, tooling, and CI gating, then push PR(s) with credit to original authors. Close the dead/stale/dependabot PRs with rationale. See work through to a pushed, green-where-runnable PR.

## Execution map (7 tracked tasks)

| # | Phase | Status |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Repo intelligence** — analysis + docs + skill + agent | 🔄 in progress (workflow running) |
| 2 | Per-task TODO planning docs + gating + agent assignment | ⏳ next |
| 3 | End-to-end GOAL definition | ✅ drafted above, doc to follow |
| 4 | Implement v4 base + correctness fixes + re-implemented features | ⏳ |
| 5 | Gating: eslint/prettier + unit tests + integration | ⏳ |
| 6 | Adversarial multi-lens review of the diff | ⏳ |
| 7 | Branch → push PR(s) with credits → final report | ⏳ |

## Just done this turn
- **Branch created:** `feat/serverless-v4-support` (off `master`, clean — all work lands here).
- **Launched WF1** (`serverless-plugins-intel`, run `wf_7b0b0e29-22c`): 6 parallel read-only analysts (architecture, per-package internals, conventions, testing/CI, deps/publish, v4+PR re-verification) → 4 doc-writers producing `docs/architecture.md`, `conventions.md`, `testing-and-ci.md`, `publishing-and-versioning.md`.

## Two findings already shaping the build
1. **Offline plugins have *zero* unit tests** — `ava.config.js` only globs `packages/**/test/*.js`; only `dynamodb-streams-readable` has any. Real ownership ⇒ I'll add **docker-free unit tests** for the pure logic (event extraction, ARN resolution, log shim, delete-batch dedup) so there's a fast gate independent of docker/serverless-v4-license.
2. **`npm run eslint` = lint *and* prettier** (the coorpacademy config bundles prettier), so that's my one reliable always-runnable gate.

I'll continue **automatically** when the analysis workflow completes — authoring the skill + agent + per-task plans, then implementing and pushing. You can watch live with `/workflows`.

*(No source code written this turn — branch + orchestration only, so no diff report.)*

---

## Audio Summary for Silou

Silou, the full workflow is underway. I set up seven tracked phases, created the feature branch, and launched a six-agent analysis workflow that's deep-reading the whole repo and writing the foundation docs right now. I'll keep going automatically the moment it lands — building the skill, the agent, the per-task plans, then implementing and pushing the PR.

content.subject: Step 1 — Repo intelligence: docs, rules, skill, agent
content.description: Deep-analyze the repo; extract architecture/conventions/CI/publish/plugin-pattern into docs/. Produce rules+guidelines+config. Create one Claude Code skill and one agent to support plugin work on this repo.

content.subject: Step 2 — Per-task TODO planning docs with gating
content.description: Create one TODO doc per implementation unit (v4 base, region fix, await fix, delete-batch IDs, queueName, no-streams error, retries, start-without-events, DLQ autocreate). Each binds relevant docs+skill, assigns an agent, and defines gating (eslint+prettier, unit tests, integration where feasible) and acceptance criteria.

content.subject: Step 3 — Define end-to-end GOAL for A, B, and required tasks
content.description: Write a GOAL doc that defines done-state for A (v4 PR) and B (re-implemented community PRs owned by us), plus close-list and credits, with measurable acceptance criteria and the path to merged/pushed PRs.

content.subject: Step 4 — Implement v4 base + correctness fixes + re-implemented features
content.description: On a feature branch: v4 logger migration across all 4 packages (shared log util), wide peers, v4 CI lane; correctness fixes #166 (region) + #249 (await create); re-implemented features #253, #211, #98, #100, #168, #183 as our own code. Add docker-free unit tests for touched logic.
content.activeForm: Implementing v4 base, fixes, and features

content.subject: Step 5 — Gating: eslint/prettier + unit tests + integration
content.description: Run npm run eslint (lint+format) to green. Run ava unit tests (existing + new). Run integration tests where docker is feasible; document v4 license/docker gaps honestly.

content.subject: Step 6 — Adversarial multi-lens review of the diff
content.description: Workflow: review the full diff across correctness, v4/v3 compat, lint/format, test coverage, regressions, semver/peer ranges. Fix confirmed findings.

content.subject: Step 7 — Branch, push PR(s) with credits, final report
content.description: Create branch(es), push, open PR(s) on CoorpAcademy/serverless-plugins with meticulous descriptions crediting original authors. Close-list rationale for dependabot/stale PRs. Deliver final report with PR link(s).
content.activeForm: Pushing PRs and writing final report

      },

        "lineCount": 378
      }
    ],
    "intelligence": [
      {
        "domain": "serverless-offline-* plugins: shared plugin architecture (s3, sqs, kinesis, dynamodb-streams)",
        "summary": "The four serverless-offline-{s3,sqs,kinesis,dynamodb-streams} plugins share a near-identical Serverless Framework plugin skeleton: a single class constructed with (serverless, cliOptions), four lifecycle hooks wired to serverless-offline's offline:start:* events, and a uniform start -> _mergeOptions -> _getEvents -> _createLambda -> _create{Service} -> ready/_listenForTermination -> end pipeline. Each plugin lazily dynamic-imports serverless-offline/lambda (ESM-only in v13) to instantiate the Lambda runner, merges options through a fixed five-source precedence chain, extracts the relevant event type from each function definition (resolving CloudFormation Fn::GetAtt ARNs to concrete queue/stream/table names), and registers SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers that call end(), which cleans up the runner and the service then calls process.exit(0). Behavior is gated for tests via NODE_ENV==='test' and an end(skipExit) escape hatch. The code is essentially copy-pasted across packages, with several latent bugs (this.region never set, log.warn vs log.warning, kinesis/dynamodb service files missing the log import, redundant null-then-assign field initialization, and an unused skipStart-only _create code path in s3.js).",
        "gotchas": [
          "Redundant field init: the constructor sets every field to null and then immediately reassigns cliOptions and serverless (index.js:21-28). The null pre-assignment is dead-on-arrival for those two and pointless for the rest (they could just be set when populated). Same pattern in the service classes (e.g. s3/s3.js:16-22, sqs/sqs.js:26-32, kinesis/kinesis.js:14-19).",
          "this.region is read but NEVER assigned. The service helpers construct events with this.region (sqs/sqs.js:119, kinesis/kinesis.js:87, dynamodb-streams/dynamodb-streams.js:101) but the constructors only set this.options — there is no this.region = options.region anywhere, so this.region is always undefined. The actual region lives in this.options.region.",
          "Logger method-name inconsistency: s3/s3.js:69,117 calls log.warn(...) while sqs/sqs.js:141,173 calls log.warning(...). Only one of these matches the @serverless/utils/log API surface; the other risks being a no-op or throwing depending on the log object shape.",
          "Missing log import in two service files: kinesis/kinesis.js and dynamodb-streams/dynamodb-streams.js do NOT import @serverless/utils/log and silently swallow handler errors (catch -> retry/delay with no logging: kinesis.js:91-94, dynamodb-streams.js:105-110), unlike s3.js/sqs.js which log. Failures in kinesis/dynamodb handlers are invisible.",
          "process.exit(0) is called unconditionally in end() when !skipExit (index.js:102-104). This hard-kills the process; any other serverless-offline plugins' offline:start:end hooks that run after this one will be cut off.",
          "The @serverless/utils/log dependency is used (log.debug at index.js:143, and log.warn/warning in s3/sqs services) but is NOT declared in any of the four package.json dependency lists — it relies on transitive resolution through the serverless/serverless-offline peer, which can break under strict/PnP installs.",
          "process.env.IS_OFFLINE = true assigns a boolean (index.js:39), but env vars are coerced to strings by Node; downstream code reading process.env.IS_OFFLINE will see the string 'true'. Harmless here but a footgun if compared to a boolean.",
          "Dead code path in s3.js: _create/_s3Event (s3/s3.js:84-125) builds a listener then immediately calls listener.stop() (s3.js:122) and is never invoked by the plugin — start() (s3.js:47-77) is the real listener path. _create is orphaned.",
          "The eventModules = [] + Promise.all(eventModules) pattern in start() and end() (index.js:47-53, 90-100) is over-engineered: there is only ever one service per plugin, so it never holds more than one or two promises.",
